title: Update on my Ai Quant Fund
author: eggrally
selftext:
Here is an update on my Ai Alpha Quant Fund Aka "The Terminator"

https://preview.redd.it/3xwi3fqoiy3h1.jpg?width=1968&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=5cdd209a520279d7dd4ddd28936ca7050624ea96

This is trading real money,  this is not a back test.  It daytrades,  38 trades today, 520 for the month.  It's hands off,  all I do as a fund manager is watch to make sure the bot is up and running.  The bot was flat a couple days ago, due to api issue, so I had to fix the bug.  I'm not selling anything here, no subscription, no alerts, no discord,  just here to show it can be done.

The Terminator - It doesn't experience fear, it doesn't hesitate, and it will relentlessly pursue its target until the mission is complete. 

Target is $10 Million

comments.author: Humble-Ingenuity-759

replies.body: dip buying,  the hard part is having ai do exactly how you would trade as a human

replies.body: Which link?

replies.body: There is no link

replies.author: Eye51
replies.body: I know.. you clearly mentioned you were not selling etc.. to me asking for a link was useless. I understood he wants a github link. People seem to want your work for free.

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Years of manually trading this strategy,  2 months , 12 hours a day to get it to work the way I wanted to.  If I gave it away, the edge is gone. It's like ask Jim Simon to give his fund secret away.

comments.author: Ok-Engineering1606
comments.body: how does it use ai ? which models are you using for what ? which tech stack ? where did you deploy it ? Dope and inspirational man, I want to build something like this too. Btw does it backtest constantly too to test new strategies ?

comments.author: angusslq
comments.body: - “I AM A MACHINE”

comments.author: uncth82
comments.body:
Are you using a commission free broker?  

How is your slippage? 

I have had to go back to my algorithm everyday this week because the live trading decisions aren’t matching up with the backtests, a relic of older strategies; hoping that’s fixed today; generally my slippage is close to reference/expected but got burned yesterday on an NVDA Buy that was 13bps over expected. 

How are you handling order flow/ pricing decisions, market orders or limit?

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: All Market orders, not too much slippage,  not every bot will make, I had many that failed.

comments.author: Sensitive-Start-6264
comments.body: whats your account size?

comments.author: Mysterious_Swim1923
comments.body:
Interesting project honestly because live automated trading always tends to raise questions around robustness, scalability, drawdowns, maintenance, market regime changes, and whether the edge survives outside controlled conditions. Building something hands-off that keeps operating consistently is a very different conversation from simply having a strong backtest.

Been seeing more trading discussions like this lately. There’s also a trading/investing community linked in my bio for anyone into trading ideas, learning, and strategy conversations.

replies.author: Mysterious_Swim1923
replies.body: https://discord.gg/SnYYFjcFap

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Im not giving away my code, im selling anything, clown, why would I give away a money making bot. Go make your own bot.

comments.body: Interesting. I actually advised a firm about a year ago that day traded SPY using teams of agents. I'm curious to see where this goes, especially if and when buy-the-dip no longer works.

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Not giving away my exact strategy,  been trading this strategy for many years manually,  now I just turned it into a bot that trades faster and takes more trades.  So for in June up 4k on 154 trades.

comments.author: Iamisseibelial
comments.body: So break it down, did you code a screener and have it execute trades from the screener? You using a premade screener and having it trade based on that? I am curious because I was planning on making one myself after seeing a few companies do AI assisted trading and that's not what I want.

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Those companies are all BS, it's for people with no technical background.  What you do is give your bots rules,  just like if you were trading,  if then, do this,  if it goes down a certain %, sell it.  Literally anyone can build a bot in 5 mins, will it make money, that's another story.

replies.author: Iamisseibelial
replies.body:
For sure. They definitely all are bs. That's why I wanted my own thing 

A screener took, a news tool, rules for what to look for, and the. Find a place to execute the trade. (The last part I definitely am struggling with figuring out how to / want to do it.)

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: get your broker api, have you bot connect to it, so it can execute the trade.

comments.author: ansh_dev0
comments.body: I have also tried one using ai and it watches on different markett and only trade on the market which is cam and the bot is really really cwer it doesn't take much risk and I makes smaller profits but looses everything in few bad trade what should I improve on , suggest me what should I focus on and what problem did u face while u were building it . It will be really really helpfull

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Test different stop %
